context_summary: End-to-end Drop Scan UAT surfaced a chain of pre-existing bugs that this session fixed: rclpy InvalidHandle race in _real_ensure_real_topics, tkinter 'main thread is not in main loop' in _real_set_status, visual-servo infinite re-loop on flaky cups, lack of pre-scan setup (no auto gripper-open / wrist-flip), gripper-open leaving planning group on 'gripper', and the user-identified sim/real subscription leak (Grasp Refresh after a Real Test session was pulling from /*_real topics into the sim collision scene). Three repos updated and committed. Mathematical analysis ruled OUT camera-pan-axis parallax as the cause of drop_2's 134mm² xy variance — Cy is only 5mm, predicting <1.5° pointing error which is negligible. Real cause remains unidentified — top hypotheses are PnP marker-pose ambiguity at oblique viewing angles after the 19° servo, or tracking lag between commanded pan and actual joint state during the 0.3s settle window.

completed_tasks.progress: Drop Scan completes end-to-end without crash. drop_1 cached cleanly (xy_std 1.31mm) on 1st run; drop_2 hit 134mm² xy variance (rejected); drop_0 never detected (likely outside +90→0° sweep range). Visual servo PIPELINE confirmed working (detect→compute target_pan→pan→3s avg→cache). Visual servo CORRECTNESS not yet verified (no ground-truth comparison vs Isaac Sim USD; no proof cup ends up centered after servo). Mathematical analysis showed camera-pan-axis parallax is NOT the cause of drop_2 variance (predicted error <1.5° at typical cup distances). SUMMARY.md NOT yet written.

remaining_tasks.name: Verify visual servo correctness via Isaac Sim ground-truth comparison
remaining_tasks.details: Pull cup USD prim transforms via MCP socket :8767, diff against _cached_cup_poses, screenshot mid-servo to confirm cup centering. ~1 hour.

remaining_tasks.name: Diagnose root cause of drop_2's 134mm² xy variance
remaining_tasks.details: Math ruled out parallax. Top suspects: (a) PnP marker-pose ambiguity at oblique viewing angle after 19° servo (max_movement Kalman seed gets reset); (b) tracking lag — /joint_states reports converged but wrist_flex still settling, /camera_pose drifts during 3s avg window; (c) DROP_SCAN_SETTLE_AFTER_STEP_S = 0.3s too short for SO-ARM101 weak drives. Action: instrument per-sample logging in localize_aruco for 3s window, look for bimodal clustering (PnP flip) vs first-30%-noisy-rest-clean (tracking lag) vs uniform-high (just bad geometry).

remaining_tasks.name: Investigate drop_0 detection (never seen in any run)
remaining_tasks.details: Sweep range +90°→+0° doesn't cover drop_0's pan angle. Either extend range to 180° (full hemisphere), OR confirm cup_red is at a position outside hemisphere via Isaac Sim ground-truth, OR add an explicit 'sweep both halves' mode.

remaining_tasks.details: After visual-servo correctness + variance + drop_0 issues are resolved or formally deferred.

Lego workflow discussion (user said 'after this is done')

blockers.description: drop_2 has consistent high xy variance (134 mm²) when servoing 19° from sweep_pos to target_pan. Root cause unknown — math RULED OUT parallax (Cy=5mm → <1.5° error). Top suspects are PnP ambiguity or tracking lag during the 3s averaging window.
blockers.workaround: None. Repeated runs reproduce. Next debug step: instrument per-sample logging in localize_aruco.

blockers.description: drop_0 never detected in any of the 4+ runs this session. Sweep was 90° from +90° to 0° — drop_0 might be at a pan angle outside that range (e.g., negative x side).
blockers.workaround: Increase sweep_range_deg spinbox to 180° to cover full hemisphere. Or check cup_red prim position via Isaac Sim MCP.

human_actions_pending.context: All structural Drop Scan bugs are fixed. Remaining issues are detection-pipeline quality concerns that may belong in a separate phase. User said 'before we get to that' (variance debug) and 'lets commit' — implying the structural fixes are good-enough closure point.

human_actions_pending.context: Camera publisher workaround is the Phase 13 viewport publisher (start_viewport_publisher 'wrist_camera_rgb_sim') — NOT the action graph. Action-graph publishers were never functional this session despite multiple attempts. Workspace cam was the original active viewport but we swapped to wrist for Drop Scan UAT.

decisions.decision: ArUco merged viewer uses YOLOE's pre-rendered annotated frame as base layer, with ArUco overlay applied via pixel diff against frame_raw
decisions.rationale: Reuses existing rendering work (cuboid wireframes, orientation axes from YOLOE) instead of re-exposing detection metadata cross-process. ~30 LOC vs ~80 LOC for the alternative. Topic decoupling (yoloe → /yoloe_annotated, aruco subscribes) means yoloe restart doesn't crash aruco viewer.

decisions.decision: Drop Scan Phase 0 uses HARDCODED SCAN_WRIST_ROLL_RAD = +π/2 instead of preserving user-set wrist_roll
decisions.rationale: User explicitly asked for the camera to flip to scan position before sweep starts, regardless of prior arm state. Roll +90 / Roll -90 buttons remain useful for manual debugging but no longer drive drop_scan input.

decisions.decision: Visual servo marks drop_id visited on EVERY failure path (servo fail, low samples, high variance), not just success
decisions.rationale: User: 'shouldnt keep visual servoing to the same block again and again'. Each cup gets exactly one attempt per scan. Trade-off: a transient bad sample window = no retry. Acceptable per user's strict-progress preference.

decisions.decision: _real_ensure_real_topics + _sim_ensure_sim_topics are idempotent on sub.topic_name (NOT StringVar value)
decisions.rationale: Race fix — destroy_subscription/create_subscription against MultiThreadedExecutor worker threads produces InvalidHandle crashes. Idempotency collapses the race window from once-per-scan to once-per-process. The previous always-re-subscribe was protecting against the StringVar-vs-actual-sub divergence; using sub.topic_name is more reliable AND avoids the race.

decisions.decision: Clear Cache is now a 'full system reset' — clears both real caches (_cached_*) AND sim caches (objects_data, _drop_data), removes all collision objects, resets subs to sim defaults
decisions.rationale: User explicit ask: 'clearing cache clears them all'. Before: only Real Test caches were cleared, sim subs/state remained ambiguous. After: pressing Clear Cache puts the whole GUI in a known-empty + sim-default state.

decisions.decision: Camera-pan-axis parallax is NOT the cause of drop_2 variance (math analysis)
decisions.rationale: Computed Cy = 5.1mm via URDF chain → Δθ ≈ Cy/D ≈ 1-2° at typical cup distances → <16 px off-center in 640px frame. PnP comfortable at this offset. Need to look elsewhere for variance root cause.

decisions.decision: Three commits across three repos this session: 651dee6 (isaac-sim-mcp/so-arm101), f131e48 (vla_SO-ARM101/main), e0b4e3d (aruco_camera_localizer/robosort)
decisions.rationale: Tight separation by repo + concern. Each commit message details the cross-repo wiring.

scripts/restart_aruco_localizer.sh (?? pre-existing standalone helper, committable but skipped this round to keep commits tight)
mobileclip_blt.ts (?? large model artifact, gitignore candidate, do NOT commit as-is)

context_notes: Session was largely successful — all structural Drop Scan bugs identified and fixed end-to-end with visual confirmation + log evidence. The user's mental model: drop_scan should be idiot-proof (auto-flip wrist, auto-open gripper, no infinite loops, no hanging on motion failures, clean separation between sim and real workflows). All four of those are now true. The remaining issue is *quality* of the cup detection — drop_2 sometimes has ~12mm xy_std after a large servo, which is NOT a structural bug but a tuning/diagnostic problem. Also there's an open question about whether drop_0 is reachable in the current sweep config or if the user needs to extend range_deg. The math analysis was a turning point — I had been assuming parallax was the cause and was about to suggest a parallax correction; the math showed Cy is ~5mm not ~50mm, ruling that out cleanly. Hot-reload via Ctrl+R only works after `xdotool windowactivate <wid>` first — that delivered ~9 minutes of failed scan attempts before figuring out. Worth documenting in the project CLAUDE.md.

live_infrastructure.isaac_sim: Running (PID 2092329, launched with PYTHONPATH=python3.11-build first to fix bridge rclpy mismatch). MCP socket port 8767 responsive.
live_infrastructure.control_stack: Running. /so_arm101_control_gui PID 2222311 (relaunched standalone after the earlier crash; uses the existing launch's params-file). MoveIt + RViz + ros2_control alive from the original PID 2048162 launch.
live_infrastructure.localize_aruco: Running headless background (PID 2162503/2162530). Subscribed to /wrist_camera_rgb_sim, publishes /aruco_poses_real, /drop_poses_real, /camera_annotated (merged frame).
live_infrastructure.viewport_publisher: wrist_camera_rgb_sim active via Phase 13 _ViewportCameraPublisher (active-viewport reuse). Workspace_camera_rgb_sim NOT publishing — that's the unfixed action-graph bug, separate scope.

anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: _real_ensure_real_topics on every drop_scan call destroyed bbox_sub while a worker thread was processing a callback on it → InvalidHandle: 'destruction was requested' crashes mid-spin. Crashed the GUI process twice in this session.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: Make sub-replacement functions idempotent on sub.topic_name. The destroy/create race window collapses from once-per-button-press to once-per-process. Generalize to: any cross-thread sub-lifecycle operation under MultiThreadedExecutor must either acquire a lock OR be idempotent.

anti_patterns_discovered.pattern: tk root.after from daemon thread without _gui_ready guard
anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: _real_set_status (called from _real_drop_scan_thread daemon worker) raised 'main thread is not in main loop' on any motion failure path, crashing the entire scan thread before its finally block could clean up. Cascaded into _set_yoloe_overlay's wait_for_service raising InvalidHandle on a destroyed client.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: Mirror _append_log's pattern: `if not getattr(self, '_gui_ready', False): return` before any root.after call. Wrap the call in try/except RuntimeError as belt-and-suspenders. Apply this guard to ALL helpers that schedule tk updates.

anti_patterns_discovered.pattern: visited_ids only added on success path (visual servo)
anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: When sample variance exceeded the threshold or sample count was low, drop_id was NOT added to visited_ids, so the next sweep step could re-detect the same cup and visual-servo to it again. Risk of infinite re-loop on a flaky cup.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: Mark visited on EVERY exit path (success or rejection). Trade-off: one bad window = no retry, but progress is guaranteed.

anti_patterns_discovered.pattern: Sub topics are sticky across mode-switches (sim ↔ real)
anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: Pressing Grasp Refresh after a Real Test session would clear objects_data then refill from /*_real (because _real_ensure_real_topics had flipped subs and grasp_refresh didn't reset them). The MoveIt collision scene then mixed real-mode lego positions with what user thought was sim mode. User-discovered: 'leak with the real world inference with the sim and real syncs'.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: Sim refresh commands (_cmd_grasp_refresh, _cmd_drop_refresh) MUST call _sim_ensure_sim_topics before pulling. Clear Cache resets to sim defaults. Generalize: any mode-aware command should explicitly assert which mode it's in by calling the appropriate ensure_*_topics helper.

anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: Ctrl+R hot-reload to control_gui silently failed for ~9 minutes of debugging because keys were going to the focused (terminal) window, not the GUI. Delayed verification and produced cascading false negatives.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: Always: `xdotool windowactivate $WID; sleep 0.5; xdotool key --clearmodifiers <key>`. Add to project CLAUDE.md.

anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: I diagnosed drop_2's high variance as 'camera-pan-axis parallax' for ~3 messages and was about to suggest a parallax correction. Math analysis showed Cy=5mm → <2° pointing error → ~13 px off-center in 640px frame → completely insufficient to explain 11.6mm xy_std. The parallax hypothesis was wrong.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: When diagnosing geometric problems, do the order-of-magnitude math BEFORE proposing a fix. Saves a debug cycle. The user explicitly asked for math analysis and that was the right call.
